extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a can only be set true by r4, whose guard is the full conjunction (qt_prolongation AND hepatic_impairment AND seizure_history AND heart_failure AND low_ejection_fraction). No other rule sets rec_drug_a, and the default leaves it false. Therefore rec_drug_a = true implies that 5-condition conjunction holds. Whenever that conjunction holds, qt_prolongation is true, so for rec_drug_b two rules match: r5 [priority 50] sets rec_drug_b := true, and r3 [priority 90] sets rec_drug_b := false. The priority resolution picks the highest-priority matching rule per output; 90 > 50, so r3 wins and rec_drug_b := false (no tie, no ordering ambiguity). Hence in exactly the only states where rec_drug_a can be true, rec_drug_b is forced to false. In all other states rec_drug_a is false. Thus (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is never jointly true. The mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire input space.
